functions.name: addAzureKubernetesEnvironment
functions.description: Adds an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) environment to the distributed application. This provisions an AKS cluster and configures it as a Kubernetes compute environment.
functions.remarks:
This method internally creates a Kubernetes environment for Helm-based deployment
and provisions an AKS cluster via Azure Bicep. It combines the functionality of
`AddKubernetesEnvironment` with Azure-specific provisioning.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the AKS environment resource.

functions.name: addGateway
functions.description: Adds a Kubernetes Gateway API Gateway resource to the application model, associated with the inner Kubernetes environment of the specified AKS environment.
functions.returns: A reference to the `IResourceBuilder`1` for chaining.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the gateway resource.

functions.name: addIngress
functions.description: Adds a Kubernetes Ingress resource to the application model, associated with the inner Kubernetes environment of the specified AKS environment. The ingress generates a `networking.k8s.io/v1 Ingress` resource in the Helm chart output at publish time.
functions.remarks:
This method delegates to the inner `KubernetesEnvironmentResource` of the AKS
environment. To use an AKS-specific ingress controller (e.g., Azure Application Gateway
for Containers), call `WithIngressClass` with the
appropriate class name.
functions.returns: A reference to the `IResourceBuilder`1` for chaining.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the ingress resource.

functions.name: addNodePool
functions.description: Adds a node pool to the AKS cluster.
functions.remarks:
The returned node pool resource can be passed to
`WithNodePool``1` on compute resources to schedule workloads on this pool.
functions.returns: A reference to the `IResourceBuilder`1` for the new node pool.
functions.signature: addNodePool(name: string, vmSize?: string, minCount?: number, maxCount?: number): AksNodePoolResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the node pool.

parameters.name: vmSize
parameters.description: The VM size for nodes. Defaults to `Standard_D2s_v5` if not specified.

parameters.name: minCount
parameters.description: The minimum node count for autoscaling. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: maxCount
parameters.description: The maximum node count for autoscaling. Defaults to 3.

functions.name: withContainerRegistry
functions.description: Configures the AKS environment to use a specific Azure Container Registry for image storage. When set, this replaces the auto-created default container registry.
functions.remarks:
If not called, a default Azure Container Registry is automatically created.
The registry endpoint is flowed to the inner Kubernetes environment so that
Helm deployments can push and pull images.
functions.returns: A reference to the `IResourceBuilder`1` for chaining.

parameters.name: registry
parameters.description: The Azure Container Registry resource builder.

functions.name: withSubnet
functions.description: Configures the AKS cluster to use a VNet subnet for node pool networking. Unlike `WithDelegatedSubnet``1`, this does NOT add a service delegation to the subnet — AKS uses plain (non-delegated) subnets.
functions.returns: A reference to the `IResourceBuilder`1` for chaining.

parameters.name: subnet
parameters.description: The subnet to use for AKS node pools.

functions.name: withSubnet
functions.description: Configures a specific AKS node pool to use its own VNet subnet. When applied, this node pool's subnet overrides the environment-level subnet set via `WithSubnet`.
functions.returns: A reference to the `IResourceBuilder`1` for chaining.

parameters.name: subnet
parameters.description: The subnet to use for this node pool.

functions.name: withSystemNodePool
functions.description: Replaces the default system node pool with a customized configuration.
functions.remarks:
Every AKS cluster requires exactly one system node pool for hosting system pods.
By default, the system pool uses `Standard_D2s_v5`. Use this method to change
the VM size when the default SKU is not available in your subscription or region.
Calling this method multiple times replaces the previous system pool configuration.
functions.returns: A reference to the `IResourceBuilder`1` for chaining.
functions.signature: withSystemNodePool(vmSize?: string, minCount?: number, maxCount?: number): AzureKubernetesEnvironmentResource

parameters.name: vmSize
parameters.description: The VM size for system pool nodes. Defaults to `Standard_D2s_v5` if not specified.

parameters.name: minCount
parameters.description: The minimum node count for autoscaling. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: maxCount
parameters.description: The maximum node count for autoscaling. Defaults to 3.

functions.name: withWorkloadIdentity
functions.description: Enables or disables workload identity on the AKS environment, allowing pods to authenticate to Azure services using federated credentials.
functions.remarks:
This ensures the AKS cluster is configured with OIDC issuer and workload identity enabled.
Workload identity is automatically wired when compute resources have an `AppIdentityAnnotation`,
which is added by `WithAzureUserAssignedIdentity` or auto-created by `AzureResourcePreparer`.
functions.returns: A reference to the `IResourceBuilder`1` for chaining.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: `true` to enable workload identity (the default); `false` to disable it.

capabilities.name: withSubnet
capabilities.description: Configures a specific AKS node pool to use its own VNet subnet. When applied, this node pool's subnet overrides the environment-level subnet set via `WithSubnet`.
capabilities.returns: A reference to the `IResourceBuilder`1` for chaining.

parameters.name: subnet
parameters.description: The subnet to use for this node pool.
